gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف أو مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (47/ 4)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف أو مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (47/ 4)
text: لِلْقَائِمِ غَيْبَتَانِ يَشْهَدُ فِي إِحْدَاهُمَا الْمَوَاسِمَ يَرَى النَّاسَ وَ لَا يَرَوْنَهُ .

en.hubeali: Al Husayn Bin Muhammad, from Ja’far Bin Muhammad, from Al Qasim Bin Ismail Al Anbary, from Yahya Bin Al Musna, from Abdullah Bin Bukeyr, from Ubeyd Bin Zurara,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘For Al-Qaim<sup>asws</sup> there would be two Occultations. In one of these, he<sup>asws</sup> would be attending the seasons (of Hajj). He<sup>asws</sup> would see the people but they would not be seeing him<sup>asws</sup>’.
en.sarwar: 12. Al-Husayn ibn Muhammad has narrated from Ja’far ibn Muhammad from al-Qasim ibn ’Isma‘il al-Anbari from Yahya ibn al- Muthanna from ‘Abdallah ibn Bukayr from ‘Ubayd ibn Zurara from abu ‘Abdallah (a.s.) who has said the following. “Al-Qa’im (the one who rise with Divine Authority) will disappear from the public sight twice. In one of them he will attain Hajj. He will see the people but they will not see him.”
